title: Unbound SHELL variable causes immediate exit under set -u before fallback applies
reasoning: Line 4 enables 'set -u'. Line 7 expands ${SHELL##*/}. In environments where SHELL is not set (minimal Docker containers, CI runners, cron jobs), bash raises 'SHELL: unbound variable' and exits immediately. The fallback on line 8 (SHELL_NAME="${SHELL_NAME:-sh}") never executes because the script already terminated. SHELL_NAME is later used in the PATH hint (line 216), so a user in such an environment gets no installer output at all.
reproduction: Run: env -u SHELL bash scripts/install.sh (or pipe via curl in a container without SHELL set). The script exits with 'SHELL: unbound variable' before any work is done.
recommendation: Replace lines 7-8 with: SHELL_NAME="${SHELL:-sh}"; SHELL_NAME="${SHELL_NAME##*/}". This provides the default before the parameter expansion that would trigger set -u.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included for this feature; reproducing requires unsetting the SHELL environment variable, which is uncommon on developer workstations.
suggestedRegressionTest: Shell test: env -u SHELL bash -n scripts/install.sh (syntax/parse), plus a functional test running the script with SHELL unset in a stubbed environment, asserting it does not exit with 'unbound variable'.
minimumFixScope: Change line 7 to SHELL_NAME="${SHELL:-sh}" and line 8 to SHELL_NAME="${SHELL_NAME##*/}".
